item.title: The Men Who Want Women to Be Quiet
item.description: A virulent form of misogyny has become the single most important force holding together the American right.

item.title: The Richest Cat in the World
item.description: Did Karl Lagerfeld really leave millions to his blue-cream Birman, Choupette?

item.title: The Night My Marriage Fell Apart
item.description: In the span of about 20 minutes, I lost my wife, my best friend, and my grip on life. Finding it again took a lot longer.

item.title: The Absurd World Cup
item.description: FIFA’s president calls football a great unifier, but is it a match for the chaos of 2026?

item.title: How America Gave Up on Its Own History
item.description: Unable to agree on how to interpret the American story, the country’s schools, universities, and political institutions have stopped trying to tell it at all.

item.title: The Americans Shelling Out Five Figures for a Coat of Arms
item.description: A nation founded on the rejection of aristocratic traditions still yearns for them.

item.title: Being Black in Pete Hegseth’s Military
item.description: The secretary of defense is sending the message that Black service members are not welcome.

item.title: How Britain Became as Poor as Mississippi
The Boeing 747 Begins Its Final Descent
item.description: The jet was perhaps the pinnacle of American engineering excellence. Its retirement signals an end to an era of American culture—and ambition.

item.title: The Whimsy and Heartbreak of America’s Garage Sales
item.description: For $100, I bought bric-a-brac that explains a nation.
